narrative: Over the 30-day window the engine closed 10 positions: 20% win rate, -6.2% average return, on a BTC chop tape that bled $77K to $73K with Fear & Greed in Extreme Fear throughout. The dominant loss pattern is LONGs entered on Peak-phase narratives that suffered mid-hold sector rotation — three AI-compute names (AKT, RENDER, TAO) and one RWA name (ONDO) lost on regime flip rather than asset-mechanics failure. The two wins came from the opposite mode: a SHORT into smart-money-exit legacy bleed (BCH +13.17%) and the single highest-confluence LONG with 7 of 10 criteria firing (NEAR +2.45%).
insights: Peak-phase LONG continuations dominate the loss column. Four of six LONG losses — ONDO, AKT, RENDER, TAO — share the same shape: entered on a sector at Peak with the narrative still rated as a ride, then the held sector either rotated within (RWA flowed to tokenized stocks and ETFs, bypassing ONDO) or inverted entirely (AI-compute moved from sweeping 7 of 8 winner slots on 2026-05-26 to leading the losers by 2026-05-28). V2 should treat a wide sector-sweep on a Peak-phase narrative as a peaking print, not a continuation green-light, and cap concurrent exposure to a single sector at two positions.

`pattern_tag_supports` and `market_regime_aligned` show the strongest signed edges in the moderate-n band: pattern_tag_supports +28.6% edge (n_fired=7, 28.6% WR vs 0% when missing), market_regime_aligned +25.0% edge (n_fired=8, 25% WR vs 0% missing). Both winners (NEAR, BCH) fired both. All three trades that missed pattern_tag_supports (AKT, RENDER, TAO) lost. Early signal: structural perps-pattern confirmation looks near-mandatory in V1 sample, n=10 cautioned.

`narrative_phase_aligned` shows NEGATIVE edge in this sample (-19.0%, n_fired=7, 14.3% WR fired vs 33.3% when missing). The cleanest win (BCH +13.17%) fired no narrative criterion at all. The signal is suggestive at this sample size: in V1, narrative-aligned entries clustered at Peak phase and lost on regime rotation. V2 should not treat narrative_phase_aligned as a positive criterion until the phase context (Rising vs Peak vs Fading) gates the weighting — Rising should add weight, Peak should subtract it.

RIDE-then-loss rate is 65% (13 of 20 RIDE calls landed on positions that ultimately did not make money). Only two trades got a CLOSE call before invalidation breached (AKT and RENDER both pre-empted stops by ~2% on a sector-thesis break). The eval cadence tracks invalidation levels well but undervalues sector-regime degradation mid-hold. V2 should make a narrative-tracker phase change an explicit re-evaluation trigger between scheduled evals, not something the daily slot picks up by chance.

The two SHORTs taken from watchlist-promoted setups (ZEC, GENIUS) both lost (-7.33% and -24.11%); the only SHORT taken directly without a watchlist stage (BCH) won +13.17%. n=3 caution applies, but the pattern is structural: SHORTs into actively-tracked narratives got run over by reassembly — Privacy/ZK flipped Fading to Rising the same day ZEC stopped out, GENIUS reactivated with a fifth distinct name-farm contract clearing the breakout gate. SHORTs into narrative-vacuums with smart-money already exited (BCH) ran clean to horizon. V2 should treat SHORT entries on actively-tracked narratives as higher-risk than SHORTs into narrative-empty bleeders.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: Top-trader long/short collapsed -1.43 over 7d (the heaviest smart-money exit in the perps universe that day), open interest dropped 9.88% in the same week (no leverage rebuild), funding -0.0336%/8h sat well below the 7d average of -0.0127% (continuation positioning, not a flush awaiting bounce), and no narrative carrier defended the bid.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: Nothing material — the position ran clean through to the 7d horizon, MFE +14.12% on the green relief tape of 2026-05-29, closed at +13.17% when BCH still printed -10.5% on horizon day.
lessons: SHORTs into legacy alts with smart-money already exited, funding in continuation territory, and no narrative defense are the V1 sample's cleanest expectation profile. V2 should weight this structural combination higher than the average SHORT.

Patience held: BCH was absent from the 25-asset perps slice and from token-movers for five straight evaluation sessions while the thesis worked underneath. Absence-of-signal on a structural short is not a thesis-break — V2 should not treat low-attention as a reason to close early.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: An accumulation pattern resolved into a +20.72% 24h breakout on volume 4.53x average, open interest +41.79% 24h, short liquidations $3.86M (vs the p75 of $1.38M), with top-trader long/short building +0.08 over 7d and both AI agents and DePIN rated peak — seven of ten confluence criteria fired, the highest-confluence entry in the window.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: Day 2 saw MAE -12.26% almost to the $2.05 invalidation; the trade held nerve and closed at horizon expiry +2.45% rather than being ridden through the sector's next leg. Small win that earned the discipline of riding the early shake.
lessons: High-confluence breakout entries survive the day-2 shake-out as long as invalidation is structurally placed. V2 should weight MAE-recovery within the first 48h as confirmation rather than as a reason to tighten the stop.

Horizon-realized exits (3d here) on momentum-name longs leave PnL on the table when the sector keeps running. V2 could test a horizon-extension rule when the narrative phase still rates ride and price holds above entry at horizon.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: The structural signal at entry was textbook capitulation flow: -6.93% close with open interest dropping 6.43% in the same window, liquidations $498K at 2.76x the 7d p75, smart-money long/short falling -0.37 over 7 days while open interest had built +124% over the same period, funding flipping negative from a +0.0073% 7d average.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: GENIUS is a name-farm cluster token — distinct contracts under the same ticker recycling through fresh launches. On Day 2 the fifth distinct bsc contract reactivated the cluster (+9.51% 24h on $51M volume against $134k liquidity, vol/mcap clearing the breakout gate at 2.38x), invalidating the capitulation read on a supply mechanic that doesn't respect single-instrument structural signals.
lessons: Name-farm cluster tokens (GENIUS / SAOS / NOAR / GDOR / SPCX pattern documented in MEMORY.md) do not behave like single-instrument assets. Shorting them on capitulation flow is unsafe because the recycled-contract supply mechanic ignores the inferred capitulation. V2 should add an explicit exclusion (or sharply reduced confidence) for tickers flagged as name-farm clusters in the active-topics file.

Cross-feed conflict policy needs nuance: the operator-side rule 'perps positioning wins commentary when the chart confirms the structural shape' was applied here against a same-day token-call HIGH 9/10 LONG on the same name. When the instrument class is a name-farm cluster, structural perps signals are not trustworthy enough to win that conflict — V2 should treat name-farm + cross-feed-conflict as a no-trade gate.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: ONDO printed real RWA-leader signal at entry: +11.81% 24h, open interest +13.19%, re-entry to the CoinGecko trending list at #6, and RWA rated peak in the narrative tracker with a ride-with-trail call — a high-quality continuation print on the sector's named leader.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: The RWA narrative carrier rotated within the sector the same week — capital stayed in RWA but flowed into tokenized stocks ($1.2B) and ETFs ($425M), bypassing the ONDO token. Invalidation $0.41 was breached by 7% on Day 2 as the within-sector rotation drained the specific-asset bid.
lessons: Late-cycle (peak-phase) narrative continuation longs are vulnerable to within-sector carrier rotation, where the narrative stays alive but the specific token gets bypassed. V2 should add a 'carrier-check' for peak-phase entries: is this specific asset still the narrative's primary expression, or are downstream carriers (ETFs, tokenized equities, alternative protocols) absorbing the flow?

Peak-phase RIDE-with-trail is a hedge that did not save the trade — the trail was not tight enough to exit before the carrier rotation drained the bid. V2 should tighten the trail discipline on peak-phase RIDE calls to one daily-close confirmation of weakness, not waiting for the invalidation level.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: RENDER had real flow at entry: +14.2% 24h on $258M volume with vol/mcap 0.21, AI-compute peers sweeping 7 of 8 winner slots, decentralized compute rated peak with a ride call, token-call independently rated HIGH 9/10. The entry confluence was clean for a continuation print.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: The same AI-compute sector that swept the winners on Day 1 inverted to lead the losers by Day 3 — RENDER specifically led the losers at -12.5%. The sector-momentum leg snapped between sessions even though the daily close held above the $2.00 stop. The fundamental supply-burn story stayed intact while the sector flow that justified the entry rotated out.
lessons: A sector-sweep entry (X of Y winner slots) on a peak-phase narrative is a peaking-print signal as much as a continuation signal. V2 should down-weight wide sector-sweep entries when the narrative phase is already at peak, and treat narrow-leadership entries (one or two names) as cleaner continuation candidates.

RENDER was the third LONG in the AI-compute book (alongside EIGEN and AKT). Sector concentration meant one sector flush hit all three positions on the same day. V2 needs a sector-concentration cap — once two positions are open in the same narrative-tracker sector, the third entry should require materially stronger asset-mechanics or be passed.

regime_observations: BTC compressed from $77,417 (2026-05-22) to $73,314 (2026-05-29), a 5.3% bleed across the window with no day printing a green relief that held. Fear & Greed sat in Extreme Fear (22-25) the entire 7 sessions — the crowd never repriced regime change.

AI/compute narrative transitioned Peak → Fading inside the window. The sector swept 7 of 8 winner slots on 2026-05-26 and led the losers by 2026-05-28 — three of the four LONG losses (AKT, RENDER, TAO) cluster around that single 48-hour regime flip.

RWA narrative held Peak conviction in the tracker but rotated its carrier within-sector: capital flowed from ONDO-direct to tokenized stocks ($1.2B) and tokenized-equity ETFs ($425M). The specific-token bid drained while the sector narrative survived. ONDO's loss reads as a within-sector flow rotation, not a narrative failure.

Perp-DEX (HYPE / ASTER) printed a FADE → PEAK → FADE whipsaw twice in one week. HYPE broke above the prior ATH ($64.27 → $64.75) on 2026-05-29 in Extreme Fear against 9/20 breadth — a late-cycle signature print that closed the HYPE book at NEUTRAL and pulled the second-derivative ASTER to NEUTRAL as well.

Privacy/ZK was rated Fading at the ZEC SHORT entry on 2026-05-23, then promoted Fading → Rising the same day the short closed (2026-05-24) on three distinct drivers (Aleo, Ledger Private DeFi, ZK-rollup wave), then back to Fading by 2026-05-29. The narrative-tracker re-classification inside one session is the regime signal that flipped the short.
